title: eriophorum polystachion subsp. latifolium
intro: Eriophorum polystachion subsp. latifolium, also known as the broad-leaved cotton-grass, is a subspecies of the cotton-grass plant, characterized by its broad leaves and ability to grow in wet environments. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The broad-leaved cotton-grass has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, with its herbal preparations often taken in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams. Furthermore, the plant's medicinal properties have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
